title: ajuga iva subsp. pseudoiva
intro: Ajuga iva subsp. pseudoiva, also known as Bugle, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Ajuga iva subsp. pseudoiva include its potential to treat conditions such as fever, respiratory issues, and skin irritations, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and improve digestion. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
